payload.title: Repo state supports three concrete rollout conventions. Git-master should assume a branch-and-PR workflow off `main`, not direct commits on `main`; Builder should default to product-code surfaces (`src/`, `tests/`) and avoid repo docs and live `.control-tower/` operational state unless a packet explicitly targets bootstrap/runtime plumbing; Scribe should treat `docs/` as canonical durable documentation, `.control-tower/docs/state` as canonical only for Tower operational status/tasks/open questions, and `.control-tower/memory` as derived non-canonical memory. Main rollout risk is that the bootstrap is still uncurated and uncommitted on `main` while the queued Scribe handoff remains blocked by Codex auth failure.
.control-tower/packets/outbox/scout-rollout-conventions.result.json
This repo already signals a branch-and-PR workflow on top of `main` through multiple `codex/*`, `copilot/*`, and `claude/*` branches merged back to `main`.
payload.summary: Inferred from ResultPacket memory_worthy entry: This repo already signals a branch-and-PR workflow on top of `main` through multiple `codex/*`, `copilot/*`, and `claude/*` branches merged back to `main`.
rationale: Captured from a ResultPacket memory_worthy entry pending curation.
